rulings.Case Title: SARAH LEWIS VS. KENNETH RUNYON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Renoticed Hearing On Motion To Seal Court Records Containing Sealed Arrest Record Pursuant To Penal Code 851.91, 851.92 And California Rules Of Court Rules 2.550-2.551
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 10, 2026. Line 1. PLAINTIFF SARAH LEWIS Motion To Seal Court Records Containing Sealed Arrest Record Pursuant To Penal Code 851.91, 851.92 And California Rules Of Court Rules 2.550-2.551 is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to file a memorandum of points and authorities and declaration in support of her motion filed May 12, 2026. The Court will not review previously filed documents that were filed in support of a prior motion. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VLADIMIR SYELSKY VS. LEV KRAYMAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 10, 2026. Line 16. DEFENDANT MADISON THOMPSON, JACOB SWANSON DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer from notice of entry of order. A demurrer tests the sufficiency of the Complaint. Defendants' demurrer is based on their assertion that they are tenants of the premises. However, this is not a fact that appears anywhere on the face of the Complaint. Defendants' factual contentions raised in response to the Complaint do not make the Complaint defective. Factual issues cannot be resolved on demurrer. This matter will be heard in department 505 by the Honorable Michelle Tong at 1:30 p.m. per the order of the presiding judge. =(505/MT) Parties may appear in-person, or via Zoom Video Conference (Meeting ID: 160 4132 4666 Passcode: 872834). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. ELLIOT HOLLINGSWORTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendants Pladee Tommy Clayton'S And Georgina Marie Jenkins' Responses To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories - General, Set Two, Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, To Deem Facts Admitted And Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Pladee Tommy Clayton And Georgina Marie Jenkins In The Amount Of $585.00
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 10, 2026. Line 8.1. PLAINTIFF THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY Motion To Compel Defendants Pladee Tommy Clayton'S And Georgina Marie Jenkins' Responses To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories - General, Set Two, Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, To Deem Facts Admitted And Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Pladee Tommy Clayton And Georgina Marie Jenkins In The Amount Of $585.00 HEARING REQUIRED for all parties to appear in person to address Defendants' contest of the Court's previously published tentative ruling: GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to compel is GRANTED. Defendant Clayton shall serve complete verified responses without objection to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories-General (Set Two) and Request for Production of Documents (Set Two) directed to him, within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Defendant Jenkins shall serve complete verified responses without objection to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories-General (Set Two) and Request for Production of Documents (Set Two) directed to her, within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Motion to deem facts admitted is DENIED in its entirety, because the Defendants have served substantially compliant responses before the hearing. Sanctions are GRANTED against Defendants Clayton and Jenkins in the total amount of $585. Provided, however, that the sanctions will be waived if Defendants timely serve complete verified responses to the outstanding discovery in compliance with the first part of this order granting Plaintiff's motion to compel. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 10, 2026. Line 8.2. PLAINTIFF THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to COMPLAINT HEARING REQUIRED for all parties to appear in person to address Defendants' contest of the Court's previously published tentative ruling: GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. As to paragraphs 3 and 6 of the second Amended Answer filed April 21, 2026, Plaintiff's motion is granted and these paragraphs are stricken in their entirety. Paragraphs 3 and 6 are not drawn in conformity with the April 20, 2026 Order on Plaintiff's Demurrer to Defendants' Amended Answer, because they exceed the scope of leave to amend granted by said order. As to paragraphs 4 and 10 of the second Amended Answer filed April 21, 2026, Plaintiff's motion is denied. The arguments advanced by Plaintiff regarding these paragraphs are not grounds for a motion to strike. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ESSEX FOX PLAZA, LP VS. ABDUL RASHEED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 10, 2026. Line 11.2. DEFENDANT SARRA ZAATOUT Amended DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, subject to opposition. The Complaint fails to state a cause of action because the Notice to Pay Rent or Quit does not comply with CCP 1161(2) because it does not state the complete address of the person to whom rent payment shall be made. This defect cannot be cured by amending the Complaint. This matter will be heard in department 505 by the Honorable Michelle Tong at 1:30 p.m. per the order of the presiding judge. =(505/MT) Parties may appear in-person, or via Zoom Video Conference (Meeting ID: 160 4132 4666 Passcode: 872834). rulings.Case Title: MERCY HOUSING MANAGEMENT GROUP, INC. VS. ANDREA A. PARKER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 10, 2026. Line 13. DEFENDANT ANDREA PARKER DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer from notice of entry of order. Although the Notice to Quit does not provide the date it was served, it does provide a date certain for the termination of tenancy should the tenants fail to surrender possession as demanded: 4/17/26. From the face of the notice, an ordinary tenant would have reasonably understood that the notice period ended on April 17, 2026. (See Eshagian v. Cepeda (2025) 112 Cal.App.5th 433.) This matter will be heard in department 505 by the Honorable Michelle Tong at 1:30 p.m. per the order of the presiding judge. =(505/MT) Parties may appear in-person, or via Zoom Video Conference (Meeting ID: 160 4132 4666 Passcode: 872834). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
